displayTitle: Lumens, Lux, and Candela: The Quantities on Your Meter

beats.narration: Every lighting requirement you'll ever read boils down to three numbers. Let's meet the quantities on your light meter.

payload.title: MEASURING LIGHT
payload.sub: The three quantities behind every lighting spec.

beats.narration: Luminous flux, in lumens, is the source's total output. Illuminance, in lux, is light landing on the surface. Luminance is light leaving toward your eye.
Some procedures speak footcandles instead. One footcandle is about ten point seven six lux, so 100 footcandles is roughly 1,000 lux.
Now watch the flashlight cone. Move back, and the same lumens spread across a bigger area — so lux at the surface drops.

payload.heading: Same lumens, bigger area, lower lux
payload.note: SAME LUMENS · MORE AREA · LOWER LUX

beats.narration: So here's the rule. The spec is written in lux at the surface. Measure there, at the work — never at the lamp.

payload.sub: Verify illuminance with a calibrated meter where the inspection actually happens.
